publication.title: Training Requirements in OSHA Standards
publication.publisher: Occupational Safety and Health Administration, U.S. Department of Labor
publication.scope: Comprehensive compilation of all training requirements contained in OSHA standards across five categories: General Industry, Maritime, Construction, Agriculture, and Federal Employee Programs
publication.legal_status: Guidance document — not a standard or regulation. Does not create new legal obligations but describes mandatory safety and health standards.

legal_framework.enabling_act: Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970
legal_framework.act_purpose: To assure safe and healthful working conditions for working men and women; by authorizing enforcement of the standards developed under the Act; by assisting and encouraging the States in their efforts to assure safe and healthful working conditions; by providing for research, information, education, and training in the field of occupational safety and health.
legal_framework.general_duty_clause: Section 5(a)(1) requires employers to provide their employees with a workplace free from recognized hazards likely to cause death or serious physical harm.
legal_framework.training_obligation: Under the OSHA law, all required training must be provided by and paid for by employers.

cfr_parts_covered.29_CFR_1918: Safety and Health Regulations for Longshoring

1910.38.title: Emergency action plans
training_requirements: Employer must designate and train employees to assist in safe and orderly evacuation
Review emergency action plan with each employee when plan is developed or employee initially assigned
Review when employee responsibilities change
Review when plan is changed
minimum_plan_elements: Procedures for reporting fire or emergency
Procedures for emergency evacuation including type and exit route assignments
Procedures for employees who remain to operate critical plant operations before evacuation
Procedures to account for all employees after evacuation
Procedures for employees performing rescue or medical duties
Contact information for employees needing plan information

1910.39.title: Fire prevention plans
training_requirements: Inform employees upon initial assignment of fire hazards to which they are exposed
Review fire prevention plan parts necessary for self-protection
Employer with 10 or fewer employees may communicate orally

1910.66.title: Powered platforms for building maintenance
training_requirements: Working platforms operated only by proficient persons
Training by competent person
Written work procedures for operation, safe use, and inspection
Employer must certify training with record including: identity of person trained, signature of employer/trainer, date completed

1910.95.title: Occupational noise exposure
training_requirements: Training in use and care of all hearing protectors provided
Training program for all employees exposed at or above 8-hour TWA of 85 decibels
Training program repeated annually
Information updated consistent with changes in protective equipment and work processes

1910.119.title: Process safety management of highly hazardous chemicals
training_requirements: Initial training on process overview and operating procedures
Refresher training at least every 3 years
Training documentation with identity of employee, date of training, verification of understanding

1910.120.title: Hazardous waste operations and emergency response (HAZWOPER)
training_levels: 40-hour off-site training for general site workers
Emergency response personnel: First responder awareness, operations, hazmat technician, specialist, on-scene incident commander

1910.132.title: General requirements for PPE
training_requirements: Train each employee required to use PPE to know: when PPE is necessary, what PPE is necessary, how to properly don/doff/adjust/wear, limitations of PPE, proper care/maintenance/useful life/disposal
Retrain when: employee does not demonstrate understanding, changes in workplace render previous training obsolete, changes in types of PPE
Employer must verify training by written certification with name, date, subject

1910.134.title: Respiratory protection
training_requirements: Comprehensive training on respiratory hazards
Proper use, maintenance and limitations of respirators
Fit testing required before initial use and annually thereafter
Training in emergency situations
Training on medical signs and symptoms limiting respirator use

1910.146.title: Permit-required confined spaces
training_requirements: Training for all workers who enter permit spaces
Training for attendants, entry supervisors, rescue team members
Training before first duty assignment and before duties change
Employer must certify training: employee name, trainer signature, date

1910.147.title: Control of hazardous energy (Lockout/Tagout)
training_requirements: Authorized employees: recognition of hazardous energy sources, type/magnitude of energy, methods/means for isolation and control
Affected employees: purpose and use of energy control procedure
All other employees: prohibition against attempting to restart/reenergize locked/tagged equipment
Retraining when: change in job assignments, change in machines/equipment/processes, new hazards, inadequacies in employee knowledge

subpart_L_fire_protection.1910.155_to_1910.165: Fire suppression, detection, alarm systems training

1910.178.title: Powered industrial trucks (Forklifts)
training_requirements: Operator must be trained and evaluated before operating
Training must include: truck-related topics, workplace-related topics, requirements of the standard
Refresher training when: observed operating unsafely, involved in accident, evaluation reveals unsafe operation, assigned different truck type, workplace condition changes
Evaluation of operator performance at least every 3 years
Certification record: name of operator, date of training, date of evaluation, identity of trainer/evaluator

subpart_Q_welding.1910.252: Welding, cutting, and brazing — general requirements

subpart_R_special_industries.1910.269: Electric power generation, transmission, and distribution

1910.332.title: Training
training_requirements: Employees working on or near exposed energized parts or near enough to be a hazard
Qualified persons: skills and techniques to distinguish live parts, determine nominal voltage, clearance distances
Content: hazards of electricity, safety-related work practices, emergency procedures including CPR

1910.1025.title: Lead
training_requirements: Initial training program for employees exposed above action level
Content: specific hazards, protective measures, purpose of medical surveillance, medical removal protection
Training repeated at least annually
Materials available to Assistant Secretary and Director upon request

1910.1030.title: Bloodborne pathogens
training_requirements: Training at time of initial assignment and annually thereafter

Content: explanation of standard, epidemiology/symptoms of bloodborne diseases, modes of transmission, exposure control plan, recognition of exposure tasks, use of PPE, hepatitis B vaccine information, emergency procedures, post-exposure evaluation, signs/labels/color-coding
Additional training for HIV/HBV research laboratories
Training records maintained for 3 years

1910.1200.title: Hazard Communication (HazCom/GHS)
training_requirements: Effective information and training on hazardous chemicals at time of initial assignment
Training whenever new chemical hazard is introduced

Content: requirements of the standard, operations where hazardous chemicals are present, location/availability of written program and SDS, physical and health hazards, protective measures, explanation of labels/SDS, emergency procedures
Chemical-specific information always available through labels and safety data sheets

1910.1450.title: Occupational exposure to hazardous chemicals in laboratories
training_requirements: Employee information and training at time of initial assignment and prior to new exposure situations
Content: contents of the standard, location of Chemical Hygiene Plan, permissible exposure limits, signs and symptoms of exposure, location of reference material

subpart_B_confined_spaces.1915.12: Precautions and order of testing before entering confined/enclosed spaces

1915.89.title: Control of hazardous energy (lockout/tags-plus)
training_requirements: Authorized, affected, and other employees
Training before initial assignment and when duties change
Certification of training required

subpart_Z_toxic_substances.1915.1450: Occupational exposure to hazardous chemicals in laboratories

subpart_C_cargo_handling.1917.45: Crane training and competent person requirements
subpart_C_cargo_handling.1917.50: Certification of marine terminal material handling devices

subpart_I_working_conditions.1918.98: Qualifications of machinery operators and supervisory training

subpart_C_general_provisions.1926.20: General safety and health provisions — programs for frequent/regular inspections

1926.21.title: Safety training and education
training_requirements: Employer shall instruct each employee in recognition and avoidance of unsafe conditions
Employer shall instruct in regulations applicable to work environment to control or eliminate hazards
Employees required to handle poisons, caustics, and other harmful substances shall be instructed on safe handling and use, aware of potential hazards, personal hygiene, PPE
Employees entering confined spaces shall be instructed on nature of hazards, precautions, use of protective/emergency equipment

subpart_D_health_environmental.1926.55: Gases, vapors, fumes, dusts, and mists
subpart_D_health_environmental.1926.61: Retention of DOT markings, placards and labels

1926.62.title: Lead in Construction
training_requirements: Initial training for employees exposed above action level
Content: lead hazards, engineering controls, work practices, respiratory protection, protective clothing, hygiene facilities, medical surveillance, employee information and warning signs
Training materials available to employees and their representatives

1926.64.title: Process safety management of highly hazardous chemicals
training_requirements: Initial training on process and operating procedures
Refresher training at least every 3 years
Training documentation required

1926.65.title: Hazardous waste operations and emergency response
training_requirements: Same HAZWOPER requirements as 1910.120
40-hour, 24-hour, 8-hour refresher training levels
Emergency response training by role/responsibility

1926.454.title: Training requirements for scaffolds
training_requirements: Each employee working on a scaffold must be trained by qualified person
Content: nature of hazards, correct procedures for erecting/dismantling/moving/operating/inspecting, maximum intended load, fall protection requirements
Retraining when: performance indicates lack of skill/understanding, changes in worksite present new hazard, type of scaffold changed, fall protection system inadequately understood

1926.500_to_1926.503.title: Fall protection training
training_requirements: Training by competent person qualified in all fall protection topics
Each employee who might be exposed to fall hazards

Content: nature of fall hazards, correct procedures for erecting/maintaining/disassembling/inspecting fall protection systems, use and operation of fall protection systems, role of employees in fall protection plan, standards in Subpart M
Certification record: name of employee, date of training, signature of competent person

subpart_CC_cranes_derricks.1926.1400_to_1926.1442: Cranes and derricks in construction — operator certification and training

subpart_D.1928.57: Guarding of farm field equipment, farmstead equipment, and cotton gins

subpart_D_inspection_abatement.1960.25: Qualifications of safety and health inspectors and agency inspections

subpart_F_committees.1960.39: Agency responsibilities regarding safety and health committees

subpart_H_training.1960.54_to_1960.60: Comprehensive training requirements for federal agencies

general_obligations: Provide all required training at employer's expense
Train employees before initial work assignment
Retrain when conditions change, new hazards introduced, or employee performance indicates deficiency
Make training materials available to employees and OSHA upon request
Review plans and procedures with employees periodically
Use competent/qualified persons as trainers where required
Provide training in language and vocabulary employees can understand
documentation_requirements: Written certification records including: employee name, date of training, trainer signature/identity
Records retention per specific standard requirements (typically 3 years minimum for some standards)
Written emergency action plans (available to employees for review)

injury_illness_prevention_programs.description: Systems that substantially reduce workplace injuries and illnesses while reducing employer costs
injury_illness_prevention_programs.state_requirements: 34 states have requirements or voluntary guidelines for workplace injury and illness prevention programs

educational_materials: Online Safety and Health Topics pages
osha_resources.oti_education_centers: Nonprofit organizations authorized by OSHA to deliver occupational safety and health training — courses in English, Spanish; community outreach; youth initiatives

outreach_training_program.description: 10-hour and 30-hour classes on recognition, avoidance, abatement, and prevention of safety and health hazards
outreach_training_program.note: This is a VOLUNTARY program — does NOT meet training requirements for any OSHA standards

osha_resources.on_site_consultation: Free confidential services separate from OSHA enforcement — funded by OSHA

data_model_mapping.training_standards: Each CFR section maps to a compliance requirement with specific training obligations
